old_claim.note: Scientific Peer Review Practices provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: The 1665 errata scope predates the 1753 Committee of Papers procedure, so the later review institution cannot enable it.

source_shape.source_locator: The Royal Society dates the journal to 1665 and its Committee of Papers publication procedure to the Society's takeover in 1753.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The dated institutional history rules out making a 1753 review procedure a prerequisite for a node anchored to 1665 print correction.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. The 1665 errata scope predates the 1753 Committee of Papers procedure, so the later review institution cannot enable it.

ontology_before: printed_scientific_errata treated scientific_peer_review_practices as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: The 1665 errata scope predates the 1753 Committee of Papers procedure, so the later review institution cannot enable it.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: printed_scientific_errata no longer has scientific_peer_review_practices as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes scientific_peer_review_practices as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped printed_scientific_errata claim.
short_reason: The 1665 errata scope predates the 1753 Committee of Papers procedure, so the later review institution cannot enable it.
